sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the ascii, the board hasn't changed much visually. The shapes are still in the same positions. The colors might have changed slightly but the overall layout is the same. Let me think about what the game mechanics more carefully.
Looking at the initial state:
- Left side: black face with white dots
- Right side: charcoal shape and yellow shape
- Right edge: yellow bar
- Bottom: black bar
The right edge yellow bar (ID=3) changed from yellow to black (2 pixels). The black face (ID=4) changed from black to yellow (62 pixels). The charcoal shape (ID=5) changed from charcoal to black (40 pixels). The white dots (ID=6) changed from white to charcoal (45 pixels). The white dots (ID=7-11) remained white (1 pixel each). The yellow shape (ID=12) remained yellow (45 pixels). The bottom bar (ID=13) remained black (63 pixels).
So SPACE seems to have cycled colors. The yellow bar on the right edge changed from yellow to black, the black face changed to yellow, the charcoal shape changed to black, the white dots changed to charcoal. The white dots remained white. This looks like a color shift operation.
Let me think about what the goal is. This looks like a puzzle where I need to match colors. The goal might be to make all objects the same color, or to match the colors of the objects on the left and right sides. Or maybe to make the face and the shapes match.
Let me try pressing SPACE again with SPACE to see if SPACE cycles colors in a cycle. Let me try pressing SPACE again to see if it cycles colors again.
